originalTitle: Kenyan police fire tear gas at protest against US Ebola quarantine centre plan
title: People in Kenya Ask to Stay Safe from Sickness!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, super friends! Let's hear some news from a beautiful country called Kenya. Kenya is in Africa, and it's a place with amazing animals and kind people. Something happened there that made some grown-ups feel a bit worried. It's about staying safe and a sickness called Ebola. We are going to learn about why people were worried and what happened. It’s important to understand how people want to keep their families healthy and happy. Everyone wants to feel safe and sound, right? That’s what this story is all about – people wanting to be safe.

What is Ebola?

First, let's talk about Ebola. Ebola is a sickness, but it's not like a small cold or a sniffle that most people get sometimes. It can make people very, very sick. It makes them feel tired and can give them a fever. Doctors and nurses work super hard to help people who get Ebola. It's a very serious sickness, so everyone needs to be extra careful when it is around. Luckily, Ebola is very rare. This means it doesn't happen often, but when it does, everyone needs to be super careful to stop it from spreading. It's like a very, very bad flu, but much worse. That's why people care so much about keeping it away. Did you know? Ebola is a very rare sickness! It doesn't happen often, but when it does, doctors and nurses work incredibly hard to help sick people get better and stop others from getting sick too.

What is a Quarantine Centre?

Now, imagine if someone had a super contagious flu. To make sure other people don't get sick, sometimes they stay in a special room or a special place by themselves for a little while. This special place is called a 'quarantine centre'. It's a place where sick people can get special help from doctors and nurses. It also helps keep others safe from getting the sickness too. Think of it like a special hospital just for this one kind of sickness. It’s built to make sure the sickness does not spread to healthy people. It helps everyone stay safe. It’s a place for healing, but also a place for keeping a sickness contained, like keeping a special, precious toy safe in its box so it doesn't get lost or broken. Did you know? Doctors and nurses who work in these special places wear special suits! They often cover their whole body, like an astronaut suit! This is to keep them super safe from the germs and sickness.

Why Were People Worried?

So, in Kenya, some grown-ups heard about a plan. A plan to build a special place, a 'quarantine centre', for people who might get Ebola. This centre was going to be built with help from a country far away called America, or the 'US'. But many people in Kenya were worried about this plan. They had two big worries. Their first worry was about getting sick. Many people in Kenya were worried about their families and friends getting sick. They thought, 'What if someone from this special centre accidentally makes other people outside sick?' They wanted to make sure everyone in their town and other towns stayed healthy and safe. They were worried the sickness might spread from the centre to their homes, schools, and markets. It's like when you share a toy, but then someone might get your germs. They wanted to keep the germs far, far away from their loved ones and homes. Their second worry was about not knowing enough. The people also felt a bit sad because they didn't know much about this new special centre. They felt like the 'government' (which is like the big grown-ups who help run the country) didn't tell them everything. They wanted to understand how it would work. They wanted to know the plans for keeping everyone safe. They asked, 'How will it be built? Where exactly? And what are the rules to keep everyone safe?' It's like if your parents planned a big surprise, but didn't tell you any of the important details, and you got a bit worried because you didn't know what to expect. They wanted to be included in the plans. Did you know? It's very important for everyone to talk and listen to each other, especially when big plans are being made! Good communication helps everyone understand and feel safe.

What Happened at the Protest?

Because the grown-ups were so worried and felt like they weren't being heard, they decided to show how they felt. They gathered together in a big group. This is called a 'protest'. When people protest, they usually walk together and hold signs to show their feelings and ideas. They wanted the government to listen to them. They wanted to share their concerns about the centre. They walked and held signs that said things like 'We want safety!' But then, something a bit uncomfortable happened. Some police officers, who are there to keep order and help everyone stay safe, used something called 'tear gas'. It's not real gas that makes you sick forever, but it's a special kind of smoke that can make your eyes watery and sting a little bit. It makes people cough and feel uncomfortable, and it often makes people want to go home. It's not a nice thing to happen when people are just trying to talk and share their worries. It made the protest stop, but it didn't stop the worries people had in their hearts. Did you know? People in many countries have the right to share their ideas and feelings peacefully. It's called freedom of speech! It means people can say what they think without fear.

What Happens Next?

Now, everyone hopes that the grown-ups in Kenya will talk more. The people want the government to listen to their worries and fears about the special centre for Ebola. They want to make sure everyone is safe and happy in their homes and communities. It's like when friends have a problem, they sit down and talk until they find a good solution together. We hope the leaders and the people can find a way to keep everyone safe and calm, and make plans that everyone agrees with. Listening is the first step to finding a good solution for everyone. They all want what is best for Kenya and its people. Did you know? Good leaders always try to listen to the people they lead. It helps everyone feel respected, safe, and heard, and builds trust between people and their leaders!

originalTitle: Blow for Kenya's ex-deputy president as court upholds his impeachment
title: Blow for Kenya's ex-deputy president as court upholds his impeachment
content: The decision weakens Rigathi Gachagua's bid for the presidency, although his lawyers have vowed to challenge the ruling.

title: Somali referee Artan barred from entering US

originalTitle: Sea drone rescues US army helicopter crew near Strait of Hormuz
title: A Super Robot Boat Saves Brave Pilots!
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa! I have a super exciting and important story to share with you today. It's about a big flying machine, brave people, and a clever robot boat! Get ready for an adventure! We always hear about amazing things happening around the world. Today's story shows us how new ideas and smart machines can help people when they are in trouble. It’s like a real-life superhero story! This happened far, far away, over a very big ocean. But the message is for all of us: never give up, and help is always on the way!

Oh No! A Big Flying Machine Falls Down!

Imagine a very big flying machine. It's called a helicopter. Helicopters are special because they can fly straight up and down, and they have giant spinning blades on top! They are like a super speedy taxi in the sky. This big helicopter was an "Apache" helicopter. It belonged to the army from a country called America. The army has many brave men and women who help keep people safe. Sometimes they use these special helicopters. On a day not long ago, this big Apache helicopter was flying over a very, very big sea. Suddenly, something went wrong! Oh dear! The helicopter started to fall down, down, down towards the water. Splash! It landed in the big, blue sea. This was a very scary moment for the people inside. **Did you know?** Helicopters are different from airplanes! Airplanes need a long runway to take off, but helicopters can lift straight up from almost anywhere!

Brave Pilots Need Help!

Inside the helicopter were two brave people. They are called pilots. Pilots are very clever people who know how to fly these amazing machines. They are trained to be very strong and calm, even when things are difficult. When their helicopter fell into the sea, these two pilots needed help. They were in the water, far from land. It was a big, big ocean all around them. They needed someone to find them quickly and make sure they were safe. It's like when you are playing outside and get a little bit lost, and you need a grown-up to find you. But this was much bigger and in the sea! **Did you know?** Being a pilot takes many years of special school and practice! They learn everything about flying and how to fix problems.

Meet Our Super Robot Boat!

Now, this is where our super hero comes in! It's not a person, but a very, very smart machine! It's called a "sea drone." What is a drone? Well, you might have seen small flying toys that look like tiny airplanes with many propellers. Those are drones! They can fly without a person inside. A "sea drone" is like that, but it's a boat! It's a special boat that can drive itself in the water. No one sits inside to steer it. It has special cameras and computers that tell it where to go and what to do. Isn't that amazing? This sea drone was patrolling, which means it was moving around the ocean, watching and looking for things. It's like a robot guard for the sea. **Did you know?** Some drones are so small they can fit in your hand! Other drones are very big and can carry heavy things.

The Hero Robot Swims to the Rescue!

So, the two pilots were in the big ocean, waiting for help. And then, guess what? The super smart sea drone came! This robot boat saw the pilots. Its smart computers knew exactly what to do. It quickly went to where the pilots were floating in the water. It went right up to them, like a friend coming to say hello. The pilots were so happy to see it! Imagine seeing a friendly robot coming to help you when you are in trouble. The sea drone stayed with them. It helped them stay safe until a bigger boat could come. It was like a floating life raft and a friendly helper all in one! It kept them warm and safe, and it made sure they weren't alone. What a clever robot! **Did you know?** Robots can do many helpful jobs! They can clean houses, build cars, and even help doctors in hospitals!

Safe and Sound!

Soon, a bigger boat arrived. This bigger boat was also from the army. Because the sea drone was there, the bigger boat knew exactly where to go to find the pilots. It made the rescue much faster and easier. The two brave pilots were pulled out of the water. They were safe! Thanks to the smart robot boat, they were found quickly and were okay. Everyone was so happy! It was a fantastic team effort. The pilots were brave, and the robot boat was super smart and helpful. This story shows us how important it is for everyone, and even robots, to help each other. **Did you know?** When people are rescued from the sea, they often get warm blankets and hot drinks to help them feel better!

Robots Are So Smart!

This is a wonderful example of how new ideas and technology can save lives. Robots are not just for playing games; they can do real and important work in the world. Sea drones can do many things. They can help watch the oceans, look for things that are lost, and even help clean up pollution. And, as we saw in our story, they can be heroes in a rescue! Scientists and engineers work very hard to make these amazing machines. They think of ways for robots to make our world a safer and better place. **Did you know?** Some robots can even explore outer space, like Mars! They send pictures and information back to us on Earth.

Always Helping Each Other!

So, there you have it! A big flying machine falls into the sea, two brave pilots need help, and a super robot boat comes to save the day! This story makes us feel happy because it shows how people and smart machines can work together to help. It reminds us that even when scary things happen, there are always helpers. Let's always remember to be kind and help each other, just like our robot hero helped the pilots. And let's be excited about all the new, smart inventions that make our world a better place for everyone, everywhere, even here in Africa! What an amazing adventure!
summary: A smart robot boat saved two brave pilots after their big flying machine, a helicopter, fell into a far-off sea.
whyMatters: This story shows us how new robot helpers can save people when they are in big trouble, making the world safer.

originalTitle: I have right papers and visa - barred referee Artan
title: I have right papers and visa - barred referee Artan
content: Somali referee Omar Artan says he was subjected to an 11-hour immigration interview before being denied entry to the United States for the World Cup despite holding the "right papers" and "right visa".
